summary: P9.C4 v1 hot/full-tile Q4_K selected dual prototype is correct but slower than retained 32x16. Full-tile hot kernel alone is promising (~49 ms / VGPR 64), but complete fallback tail/cold work costs ~17-20 ms, so total Q4 replay rises to ~65-66 ms vs retained ~59.9 ms. Sidecar/reduced-decode work remains necessary.

correctness.evidence: tests/test_gguf_q4_k_selected_wmma_prefill.py::test_p9_c4_q4_k_hot_fulltile_hybrid_matches_cpu_selected_reference passes; full Q4 selected fixture file passes (13 tests).

decision.reason: Hybrid requires a second fallback launch. The full-tile kernel reduces VGPR to 64 and removes row-valid checks, but tail/cold fallback over row/expert grids dominates enough to erase gains.
decision.next: Proceed to P9.C5 sidecar/reduced-decode prototype. Full-tile kernel can be reused only if paired with a much more compact tail list and/or sidecar.
